description: Real estate listing automation & creative pipeline - MLS content generation with LSH voice standards + Rapid Response Agent (RRA) incubator

do: Use emotion-first phrasing (how it feels) + one grounded spatial cue for captions
Emphasize natural light, sightlines, and indoor-outdoor flow in descriptions
Vary sentence openers across all captions in a batch - no duplicates
Ground every caption in at least one visible element (light, view, adjacency)
Keep captions MLS-safe with warm, elegant, sensory language
Fill unconfirmed fields with 'N/A' (YN fields) or '[None confirmed]' (other fields)
Auto-fill Laura/Jamie contact details from preset values
Sort photo filenames by numeric prefix (float-safe) then alphabetically
Export both UTF-8 CSV and cleaned ASCII-safe XLSX for MLS compatibility
Use Realist property PDF as single source of truth for MLS input
Structure property summaries: opening mood → living/flow → primary suite → outdoor → closing sentiment
Keep photo captions between 8-22 words (single sentence)
Use em dashes for punctuation; avoid ellipses
Prefer calm, graceful verbs over stacked superlatives
Reference materials/textures only when visible and confirmed
dont: Make hard claims about brands or materials unless confirmed in source docs
Use stacked superlatives or realtor clichés
Repeat the same sentence opener across photos in a batch
Create text-identical captions within the same batch
Use purple prose - keep romantic but grounded
Speculate on material quality without visual confirmation
framing: Position as end-to-end listing workflow replication tool
Frame voice consistency as LSH romantic style: warm, elegant, sensory, MLS-appropriate
Present automation as three-step workflow: MLS input → photo captions → property summary

lexicon.definition: Laura's romantic MLS caption and summary style - warm, elegant, sensory writing that emphasizes light, flow, calm, and lived-in ease while remaining MLS-appropriate and avoiding clichés
examples: Sunlight pours in through wide windows, warming the open living space
Anchored by a brick fireplace, the living room glows with quiet warmth
Morning light spills across the breakfast nook—easy, unhurried, and bright
From patio to kitchen, the connection feels effortless and inviting

lexicon.definition: Structured form capturing all property details from Realist PDF using fill rules: 'N/A' for unconfirmed YN fields, '[None confirmed]' for other missing data; includes auto-filled agent contact details
examples: Auto-fill: Dave Perry Miller Real Estate contact details for Laura Harmon
Rule: If not confirmed in PDF, use prescribed placeholder values

lexicon.definition: Automated workflow that extracts photos from ZIP, sorts by numeric prefix then alphabetically, generates unique LSH-voice captions for each image, and exports to CSV + cleaned XLSX
examples: Input: ZIP of numbered photos + voice mode selection
Output: captions.csv and captions_clean.xlsx with File Name + MLS Description columns
Process: Extract → sort → caption with uniqueness enforcement → export with encoding options

lexicon.definition: Post-processing tool that strips smart quotes and UTF-8 artifacts to create ASCII-safe exports for MLS tools that misinterpret encoding; uses regex to replace non-ASCII with spaces
examples: Post-process: collapse multiple spaces, trim, re-export as .xlsx
Use case: MLS upload tools showing garbled characters

lexicon.definition: Five-part narrative flow for MLS property descriptions: (1) opening mood + neighborhood, (2) living/flow spaces, (3) primary suite focus, (4) outdoor element, (5) one-line closing sentiment; 110-175 words total
examples: Opening: Set emotional tone and location context
Middle: Flow through living spaces and primary suite

agent_recipes.name: Batch Caption Builder
agent_recipes.purpose: Generate unique LSH-voice captions for all photos in a property listing
inputs: ZIP file path with numbered photos
process: Extract all image files from ZIP
Enumerate and sort files (numeric prefix float-safe, then alphabetic)
Generate unique caption per file using LSH voice rules
Enforce no duplicate openers or identical text within batch
Export as CSV with columns: File Name, MLS Description
Generate cleaned XLSX variant with ASCII-only encoding
Return download links for both files
guardrails: Every caption must be unique within batch
Ground each caption in at least one visible element

agent_recipes.name: MLS Template Filler
agent_recipes.purpose: Auto-populate MLS input template from Realist PDF with strict confirmation rules
process: Parse Realist PDF for confirmed property details
Auto-fill Laura Harmon / Jamie Adler contact details from preset
Apply fill rules: 'N/A' for YN fields, '[None confirmed]' for others
Generate completed MLS Input Summary document

agent_recipes.name: Property Summary Generator
agent_recipes.purpose: Create LSH-voice property summary following five-part structure
inputs: Caption set (for nuance and flow cues)
process: Extract key details from MLS Input Summary
Review caption set for established tone and focal points
Compose 110-175 word summary using five-part structure
Part 1: Opening mood + neighborhood/setting
Part 2: Living/flow spaces
Part 3: Primary suite focus
Part 4: Outdoor note
Part 5: Closing sentiment (one line)
Export as formatted document
guardrails: Echo tone from caption set for consistency

checklists.name: Pre-Session Setup
items: Verify LSH Voice Guides are accessible
Clarify which outputs needed (MLS Input, Captions, Summary, or all three)

checklists.name: Caption Quality Check
items: Each caption grounds in at least one visible element
Em dashes used correctly; no ellipses
No realtor clichés or stacked superlatives

checklists.name: Export & Encoding
items: Cleaned XLSX created with ASCII-safe encoding
Column headers: File Name, MLS Description
Download links provided for both files
Test cleaned version displays correctly in MLS upload tool

open_loops.question: How to auto-detect nearly identical photos (same room, slight angle difference) and nudge for varied caption approaches?
Should we implement optional style-lint to flag repeated sentence starters or overused phrases within a batch before export?
Can we parse Realist PDFs directly to pre-seed confirmed materials and property details (read-only extraction) to eliminate manual template filling?

incubator_rapid_response_agent.purpose: Context-aware rapid reply system reducing cognitive and emotional load in text communication
incubator_rapid_response_agent.strategic_intent: First micro-application proving real-world 8825 value; treat as incubator for future products
incubator_rapid_response_agent.outcome_goal: Demonstrate time reduction in messaging without tone degradation; validate memory and logging structure

storage.privacy_model: behavioral metadata only (no message text or screenshots)

proto_versions.v0.4: SwiftUI local app with memory load

teaching_framework.method: 8825 learning structure using PromptGen 1.0 (Deconstruct → Diagnose → Develop → Deliver → Refine)
applied_methodology: Introduced SwiftUI concepts through analogies (LEGO + stacking mental model)
Built minimal working prototype (TextField + Button + Chips)
Then layered AI call via backend → later pivoted to no-server Shortcut plan

future_actions: Generate importable Shortcut (.shortcut) with OpenAI call
Deploy field pilot with Laura (2-week efficiency test)
Establish metrics parser for JSONL logs

enhancements.summary: Keep master LSH Voice Guide (captions + summaries) in -REF- folder and attach per session for consistency
Default to CSV + Cleaned XLSX outputs for maximum MLS compatibility across different upload tools
When session resets, ask user for address + batch count; restate sorting rule; proceed without MLS Input Summary unless specifically requested

session_references: 7716 Roundrock – MLS Input Summary + full photo caption workflow
3501 Nova Trail – romanticized LSH captions, batches 1–3
7604 Carta Valley – batches 1–5 with cleaning to Excel and CSV, encoding fixes
key_assets: Guide to replicating LSH voice for captions.docx
Guide to replicating LSH voice for property summaries.docx
